{"query":"SpaceX Raptor 3 engine McGregor test campaign July 2026 status","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1207,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://starship-spacex.fandom.com/wiki/Raptor_3","title":"Raptor 3 | Starship SpaceX Wiki | Fandom","content":"The first Raptor 3 engine was spotted on 3 August 2024 at McGregor.
Numerous firings (over 300 individual tests with a cumlitive duration of over 16,000s by May 2025 ) from Raptor 3 configured stands suggested active and intensive testing of the engine, very probably with many more engines that just the few observed particularly consindering the ranging serials of up to Raptor 3 Serial Number 31.
By the 18th of November 2025, a steady flow of Raptor 3 engines were being observed, with regular truckloads of 3-4 engines leaving the McGregor test site and up to Raptor 3 serial number 68 observed, indicating steadily increasing production and testing towards the first Block 3 Raptor (and Starship) flight in early 2026.
See status and notes for more information.

Through the agreement with EchoStar, SpaceX will purchase EchoStar’s 50 MHz S-band spectrum in the US (bands known as AWS-4 and PCS-H) as well as its global MSS spectrum licenses.
There are no hazards to the surrounding communities in the Rio Grande Valley.
SpaceX has also officially adopted Boca Chica Beach through the Texas General Lands Office Adopt a Beach Program, with the responsibility of picking up litter and promoting a litter-free environment.

As part of the Commercial Crew Program, NASA astronauts Shane Kimbrough and Megan McArthur, Japanese Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) astronaut Akihiko Hoshide, and European Space Agency (ESA) astronaut Thomas Pesquet flew aboard the Dragon spacecraft on its second operational mission to the space station.

Launched atop [Falcon 9](https://www.spacex.com/vehicles/falcon-9) on May 30, 2020, [Dragon](https://www.spacex.com/vehicles/dragon) 's second demonstration flight test to and from the space station restored human spaceflight to the United States for the first time in almost a decade. On Saturday, May 30, SpaceX’s Falcon 9 launched Crew Dragon’s second demonstration [(Demo-2)](https://www.spacex.com/static/images/backgrounds/demo2patch_final.png) mission from Launch Complex 39A (LC-39A) at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida with NASA astronauts Bob Behnken and Doug Hurley aboard the spacecraft. In March 2019, SpaceX completed an end-to-end [test flight of Crew Dragon](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F-wBgsf8jWY&feature=youtu.be) without NASA astronauts onboard, making Dragon the first American spacecraft to autonomously dock with the International Space Station and safely return to Earth.","score":0.07802913,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.87,"request_id":"e1edeaf1-b492-4f34-8ea6-00016f3b6565"}
